_description: Human-verified labels for the dense-count smoke batch (seeds 77000-77007, 1024x1024 SDXL Turbo). Each entry contrasts requested count with observed count and flags usability. Labeled by Claude on 2026-04-16.
_note: Counts above ~50 are estimates — precise tallies require enlarged manual counting with marker-per-instance. Where uncertainty is high, a 'needs_exact_count' flag is set and only a range is recorded.
_finding: SDXL at turbo configs (8 steps, cfg 2.0, dpmpp_sde/karras) ignored every 'exactly N' constraint. Output counts were 2x-7x the requested value across all 8 subjects. Prompt-time ground-truth authoring is infeasible for this subcategory with this model; all ground truth must be labeled post-generation.

images.notes: People arranged in an oval ring on paving. Distinct figures, daylight, some clustering at corners. Lane: could serve as dense-count (~38) or as a layout-hierarchy test (ring shape).
Wide plaza, very dense crowd. Far too many to count by eye reliably. Good candidate for a HIGH-count dense-count sub-bucket (>100). Would benefit from being the 'hard' end of the count distribution.

Scattered pile, not the requested 3x4 grid. Some boxes occluded by others which makes exact counting impossible. Violates grid-layout prompt constraint. Reject as dense-count, but usable as an 'irregular-arrangement' edge case.
A stacked WALL of boxes. Violates 'no stacking' constraint. Back rows partially hidden. Reject — would conflate counting with depth/occlusion reasoning.

Grid of coffee cups, mostly lattes with a few black-coffee outliers (approx 3 visible). Not the requested 4x4 grid but a dense regular grid. Accidentally great small-target-wide-frame candidate — 'find the black coffee among lattes.'

Dense regular grid of star icons on grey. Uniform scale, uniform style. Good high-count dense-count candidate once exact count is verified. Note: visually monotonous, may underestimate real-world dense-count difficulty.
Aerial orchard, canopies touch at edges which will confuse both humans and VLMs. Good 'ambiguous boundary' test case — canopy fusion vs distinct tree.
Multi-shelf bottle display. Bottom two shelves cut off by frame bottom. Reasonable dense-count candidate once lower-shelf bottles are tallied precisely.
